intro: Ardisia intibucana, also known as Ceylon boxwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae, native to Sri Lanka and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Ardisia intibucana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ardisia intibucana is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as to support immune function.
